origin: Short head: apex of coracoid process of scapula. Long head: supraglenoid tubercle of scapula.
insertion: Posterior aspect of radial tuberosity; bicipital aponeurosis into deep fascia of forearm.
action: Flexion at elbow; supination of forearm; weak flexion at glenohumeral joint.
notes: Two-headed muscle on the anterior surface of the upper arm. Acts primarily as a supinator and powerful elbow flexor; most effective when the elbow is at 90°.

origin: Short head: coracoid process of scapula. Long head: supraglenoid tubercle of scapula.
insertion: Radial tuberosity and bicipital aponeurosis.
action: Flexion at elbow; supination of forearm.
notes: Mirror of right biceps brachii.

origin: Long head: infraglenoid tubercle of scapula. Lateral head: posterior humerus above radial groove. Medial head: posterior humerus below radial groove.
insertion: Posterior surface of olecranon process of ulna.
action: Extension at elbow joint; long head assists in adduction and extension of arm.
notes: Only muscle on the posterior compartment of the upper arm; principal extensor of the elbow.

origin: Long head: infraglenoid tubercle. Lateral head: posterior humerus above radial groove. Medial head: posterior humerus below radial groove.
insertion: Olecranon process of ulna.
action: Extension at elbow.
notes: Mirror of right triceps brachii.

origin: Anterior: lateral third of clavicle. Middle: acromion. Posterior: spine of scapula.
insertion: Deltoid tuberosity of the humerus.
action: Anterior: flexion and medial rotation. Middle: abduction. Posterior: extension and lateral rotation of arm.
notes: Large triangular muscle forming the rounded contour of the shoulder. The middle fibres are the primary abductors of the arm.

origin: Lateral clavicle, acromion, spine of scapula.
insertion: Deltoid tuberosity of humerus.
action: Abduction, flexion, extension, rotation of arm.
notes: Mirror of right deltoid.

origin: Lateral supracondylar ridge of humerus.
insertion: Lateral surface of distal radius just above the styloid process.
action: Flexion at elbow (most effective when forearm is in semi-prone position).
notes: Forms the lateral boundary of the cubital fossa. Anatomically in the posterior compartment but functionally a flexor.

origin: Lateral supracondylar ridge of humerus.
insertion: Distal radius near styloid process.
action: Elbow flexion.
notes: Mirror of right brachioradialis.

origin: Infraspinous fossa of scapula.
insertion: Middle facet of greater tubercle of humerus.
action: Lateral rotation of arm; helps stabilize glenohumeral joint as rotator cuff muscle.
notes: Part of the rotator cuff. Occupies most of the infraspinous fossa.

origin: Infraspinous fossa of scapula.
insertion: Greater tubercle of humerus.
action: Lateral rotation of arm; rotator cuff stabilizer.
notes: Mirror of right infraspinatus.

origin: Lower anterior surface of humerus.
insertion: Coronoid process and tuberosity of ulna.
action: Flexion at elbow joint (pure flexor regardless of forearm rotation).
innervation: Musculocutaneous nerve (C5, C6); small contribution from radial nerve.
notes: Lies deep to biceps brachii; the workhorse flexor of the elbow as its line of pull is purely flexion.

origin: Lower anterior humerus.
insertion: Coronoid process and ulnar tuberosity.
action: Elbow flexion.
notes: Mirror of right brachialis.

origin: Apex of coracoid process of scapula.
insertion: Middle third of medial surface of humerus.
action: Flexion and adduction of arm at glenohumeral joint.
notes: The musculocutaneous nerve pierces this muscle. Weak flexor and adductor of the arm.

origin: Coracoid process of scapula.
insertion: Medial humerus, middle third.
action: Flexion and adduction of arm.
notes: Mirror of right coracobrachialis.

origin: Clavicular head: medial half of clavicle. Sternocostal head: sternum and upper six costal cartilages. Abdominal head: aponeurosis of external oblique.
insertion: Lateral lip of intertubercular sulcus (bicipital groove) of humerus.
action: Adduction and medial rotation of arm; clavicular head flexes arm; sternocostal head extends flexed arm.
notes: Large fan-shaped muscle forming the anterior wall of the axilla. Divided into clavicular and sternocostal heads.

origin: Medial clavicle, sternum, upper costal cartilages.
insertion: Intertubercular sulcus of humerus.
action: Adduction, medial rotation, flexion and extension of arm.
notes: Mirror of right pectoralis major.

origin: Pubic symphysis and pubic crest.
insertion: Xiphoid process and costal cartilages of ribs 5–7.
action: Flexion of lumbar spine; compresses abdominal viscera; assists forced expiration.
notes: Enclosed within the rectus sheath. Three tendinous intersections give the 'six-pack' appearance in lean individuals.

origin: Outer surfaces of ribs 5–12.
insertion: Linea alba, pubic tubercle, anterior iliac crest.
action: Bilateral: flexion of trunk, compression of abdomen. Unilateral: ipsilateral lateral flexion, contralateral rotation.
notes: Largest and most superficial of the three flat abdominal muscles. Fibers run inferomedially like hands in pockets.

origin: Outer surfaces of ribs 5–12.
insertion: Linea alba, pubic tubercle, iliac crest.
action: Trunk flexion, rotation, lateral flexion.
notes: Mirror of right external oblique.

origin: Superior nuchal line, external occipital protuberance, nuchal ligament, spinous processes of C7–T12.
insertion: Lateral third of clavicle, acromion, and spine of scapula.
action: Upper: elevates and upwardly rotates scapula. Middle: retracts scapula. Lower: depresses and upwardly rotates scapula.
innervation: Accessory nerve (CN XI); proprioception from C3, C4.
notes: Large, flat, diamond-shaped muscle covering the posterior neck and thorax. Divided into upper, middle, and lower thirds with distinct functions.

origin: Spinous processes of T7–T12, thoracolumbar fascia, iliac crest, ribs 9–12, inferior angle of scapula.
insertion: Floor of intertubercular sulcus of humerus.
action: Extension, adduction, and medial rotation of arm; depression of shoulder girdle.
notes: Broadest muscle of the back. Forms the posterior axillary fold. Key muscle in climbing and rowing movements.

origin: Thoracolumbar fascia, lower spinous processes, iliac crest.
insertion: Intertubercular sulcus of humerus.
action: Extension, adduction, medial rotation of arm.
notes: Mirror of right latissimus dorsi.

origin: Outer surfaces of ribs 1–8 (or 9).
insertion: Costal surface of medial border of scapula.
action: Protracts and holds scapula against thoracic wall; upwardly rotates scapula; assists in raising arm above horizontal.
notes: Palsy of the long thoracic nerve causes 'winging' of the scapula. Visible as serrated strips on the anterolateral chest wall in muscular individuals.

origin: Outer surface of ribs 1–8.
insertion: Medial border of scapula, costal surface.
action: Scapular protraction and upward rotation.
notes: Mirror of right serratus anterior.

origin: Common origin: posterior sacrum, iliac crest, sacroiliac ligaments, spinous processes of lower thoracic and lumbar vertebrae.
insertion: Ribs, transverse processes, and spinous processes of vertebrae above (specific attachments vary by sub-muscle).
action: Bilateral: extension of vertebral column. Unilateral: lateral flexion of trunk.
innervation: Dorsal rami of spinal nerves at each level.
notes: A group of three columns (iliocostalis, longissimus, spinalis) that run the length of the back. Essential for maintaining upright posture.

origin: Posterior sacrum, iliac crest, lumbar spinous processes.
insertion: Ribs and vertebrae above.
action: Spinal extension and lateral flexion.
notes: Mirror of right erector spinae.

origin: Posterior gluteal line of ilium, dorsal ilium behind posterior gluteal line, dorsal sacrum and coccyx, sacrotuberous ligament.
insertion: Iliotibial tract (upper 3/4); gluteal tuberosity of femur (lower 1/4).
action: Powerful extension and lateral rotation of thigh; upper fibres assist abduction. Crucial for rising from a seated position, climbing stairs, running.
notes: Largest and heaviest muscle in the body. Only comes fully into action during powerful hip extension against resistance.

origin: Posterior ilium, sacrum, coccyx, sacrotuberous ligament.
insertion: Iliotibial tract and gluteal tuberosity of femur.
action: Hip extension, lateral rotation of thigh.
notes: Mirror of right gluteus maximus.

origin: Outer surface of ilium between anterior and posterior gluteal lines.
insertion: Lateral surface of greater trochanter of femur.
action: Abduction of thigh; anterior fibres medially rotate; posterior fibres laterally rotate. Critical for stabilizing pelvis during single-leg stance.
notes: Lies beneath the gluteus maximus posteriorly. Critical hip abductor—weakness causes the contralateral pelvis to drop (Trendelenburg gait).

origin: Outer ilium between anterior and posterior gluteal lines.
insertion: Greater trochanter of femur.
action: Hip abduction; pelvic stabilization.
notes: Mirror of right gluteus medius.

origin: Anterior inferior iliac spine (AIIS) and groove above acetabulum.
insertion: Base of patella and tibial tuberosity via patellar ligament.
action: Extension of knee; flexion of hip.
notes: Part of quadriceps femoris. Only biarticular quad—crosses both hip and knee. Susceptible to strain with sudden sprints.

origin: Anterior inferior iliac spine and groove above acetabulum.
insertion: Patella and tibial tuberosity.
action: Knee extension; hip flexion.
notes: Mirror of right rectus femoris.

origin: Greater trochanter, linea aspera (lateral lip), intertrochanteric line of femur.
insertion: Base of patella and tibial tuberosity via patellar tendon.
action: Extension of knee joint.
notes: Largest component of quadriceps femoris. Forms the visible bulk on the outer thigh.

origin: Greater trochanter and linea aspera (lateral lip).
insertion: Patella and tibial tuberosity.
action: Knee extension.
notes: Mirror of right vastus lateralis.

origin: Long head: ischial tuberosity. Short head: linea aspera and lateral supracondylar ridge of femur.
insertion: Head of fibula; lateral condyle of tibia.
action: Flexion of knee; lateral rotation of leg. Long head also extends hip.
innervation: Long head: tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5, S1, S2). Short head: common fibular division (L5, S1, S2).
notes: Lateral hamstring muscle. The long head is biarticular; the short head only crosses the knee.

origin: Long head: ischial tuberosity. Short head: linea aspera.
insertion: Head of fibula.
action: Knee flexion; hip extension (long head).
notes: Mirror of right biceps femoris.

origin: Medial head: posterior medial condyle of femur. Lateral head: posterior lateral condyle of femur.
insertion: Posterior calcaneus via calcaneal (Achilles) tendon.
action: Plantarflexion of foot; flexion of knee.
notes: Superficial component of the triceps surae. Biarticular—contributes to both knee flexion and ankle plantarflexion.

origin: Posterior condyles of femur.
insertion: Calcaneus via Achilles tendon.
action: Plantarflexion of foot; knee flexion.
notes: Mirror of right gastrocnemius.

origin: Posterior head of fibula, soleal line and medial border of tibia.
insertion: Posterior calcaneus via calcaneal (Achilles) tendon.
action: Plantarflexion of foot; important postural muscle for maintaining upright stance.
notes: Lies deep to gastrocnemius; forms the deeper part of triceps surae. Rich in slow-twitch fibres for sustained postural work.

origin: Posterior fibula head, soleal line of tibia.
insertion: Calcaneus via Achilles tendon.
action: Plantarflexion; postural control.
notes: Mirror of right soleus.

origin: Lateral condyle and upper two-thirds of lateral tibial surface, interosseous membrane.
insertion: Medial and inferior surface of medial cuneiform; base of first metatarsal.
action: Dorsiflexion of foot; inversion of foot.
notes: Most medial and largest muscle of the anterior compartment. Forms a palpable ridge on the shin; critical for gait clearance.

origin: Lateral tibia and interosseous membrane.
insertion: Medial cuneiform and first metatarsal base.
action: Dorsiflexion and inversion of foot.
notes: Mirror of right tibialis anterior.

origin: Sternal head: anterior surface of manubrium. Clavicular head: superior surface of medial third of clavicle.
insertion: Lateral surface of mastoid process of temporal bone; lateral half of superior nuchal line.
action: Unilateral: ipsilateral lateral flexion, contralateral rotation of head. Bilateral: flexion of cervical spine; raises sternum in forced inspiration.
notes: Landmark muscle of the neck visible as a diagonal ridge. Used clinically to assess accessory nerve function.

origin: Manubrium and medial clavicle.
insertion: Mastoid process and superior nuchal line.
action: Neck rotation and lateral flexion; forced inspiration.
notes: Mirror of right SCM.

origin: Zygomatic arch and maxillary process of zygomatic bone.
insertion: Lateral surface of ramus and angle of mandible.
action: Elevation (closing) of mandible; superficial part: protraction; deep part: retraction.
innervation: Masseteric nerve from mandibular division of trigeminal nerve (CN V3).
notes: One of the principal muscles of mastication. Can be palpated by clenching the jaw.

origin: Zygomatic arch.
insertion: Ramus and angle of mandible.
action: Jaw closure; mandible protraction/retraction.
innervation: CN V3 (mandibular division of trigeminal nerve).
notes: Mirror of right masseter.

origin: Temporal fossa and temporal fascia.
insertion: Coronoid process and anterior border of ramus of mandible.
action: Elevation of mandible; posterior fibres retract mandible.
innervation: Deep temporal nerves from mandibular division of trigeminal nerve (CN V3).
notes: Fan-shaped muscle on the side of the skull; particularly active in retraction of the jaw. Hypertrophy can be visible/palpable in bruxism.

origin: Temporal fossa.
insertion: Coronoid process of mandible.
action: Jaw elevation and retraction.
notes: Mirror of right temporalis.

origin: Medial lip of linea aspera and medial supracondylar line of femur; medial intermuscular septum.
insertion: Medial border of patella and tibial tuberosity via patellar tendon.
action: Extension of knee; pulls patella medially preventing lateral subluxation.
notes: The oblique fibres (VMO) are critical for patellar tracking. Teardrop shape visible just above the knee medially in well-conditioned individuals.

origin: Medial lip of linea aspera and medial supracondylar line of femur.
insertion: Medial border of patella and tibial tuberosity.
action: Knee extension; medial patellar stabilization.
notes: Mirror of right vastus medialis.

origin: Anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS) and notch just below it.
insertion: Upper medial surface of tibia (pes anserinus with gracilis and semitendinosus).
action: Flexion, abduction, and lateral rotation of thigh at hip; flexion and medial rotation of leg at knee.
notes: Longest muscle in the body. Runs obliquely across the anterior thigh. Produces the cross-legged sitting position. Part of the pes anserinus at the knee.

origin: Anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS).
insertion: Upper medial tibia (pes anserinus).
action: Hip flexion, abduction, lateral rotation; knee flexion and medial rotation.
notes: Mirror of right sartorius. Longest muscle in the body.

origin: Ischial tuberosity (common head with biceps femoris long head).
insertion: Upper medial surface of tibia (pes anserinus).
action: Flexion and medial rotation of knee; extension of hip.
innervation: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5, S1, S2).
notes: Medial hamstring with a long tendon that forms part of the pes anserinus. Has a characteristic cord-like tendon palpable at the medial popliteal fossa.

origin: Ischial tuberosity.
insertion: Upper medial tibia (pes anserinus).
action: Knee flexion and medial rotation; hip extension.
innervation: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5, S1, S2).
notes: Mirror of right semitendinosus.

origin: Ischial tuberosity (superomedial impression).
insertion: Posterior aspect of medial tibial condyle; also forms oblique popliteal ligament.
action: Flexion and medial rotation of knee; extension of hip.
innervation: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5, S1, S2).
notes: Medial hamstring deep to semitendinosus. Named for its broad, membranous proximal tendon of origin. Contributes to the oblique popliteal ligament.

origin: Ischial tuberosity.
insertion: Posterior medial tibial condyle.
action: Knee flexion and medial rotation; hip extension.
innervation: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5, S1, S2).
notes: Mirror of right semimembranosus.

origin: Inferior ramus of pubis and ramus of ischium.
insertion: Upper medial surface of tibia (pes anserinus).
action: Adduction of thigh; flexion and medial rotation of leg at knee.
notes: Thin, strap-like superficial muscle of the medial thigh. Contributes to the pes anserinus alongside sartorius and semitendinosus.

origin: Inferior pubic ramus.
insertion: Upper medial tibia.
action: Thigh adduction; knee flexion and medial rotation.
notes: Mirror of right gracilis.

origin: Adductor part: ischiopubic ramus. Hamstring part: ischial tuberosity.
insertion: Adductor part: linea aspera and medial supracondylar line. Hamstring part: adductor tubercle of femur.
action: Adduction of thigh (adductor part); extension of thigh (hamstring part).
innervation: Adductor part: obturator nerve (L2–L4). Hamstring part: tibial division of sciatic nerve (L4).
notes: Largest adductor of the thigh with two functionally distinct parts. The adductor hiatus in its tendon transmits the femoral vessels to the popliteal fossa.

origin: Ischiopubic ramus and ischial tuberosity.
insertion: Linea aspera, medial supracondylar line, adductor tubercle.
action: Thigh adduction (adductor part) and extension (hamstring part).
innervation: Obturator nerve and tibial sciatic division (L2–L4).
notes: Mirror of right adductor magnus.

origin: Head and upper two-thirds of lateral fibula.
insertion: Plantar surface of medial cuneiform and base of first metatarsal.
action: Plantarflexion and eversion of foot; supports transverse and lateral arches.
notes: Forms the lateral compartment of the leg along with fibularis brevis. Its long tendon wraps under the foot to support the medial arch.

origin: Head and upper two-thirds of lateral fibula.
insertion: Medial cuneiform and base of first metatarsal.
action: Foot plantarflexion and eversion.
notes: Mirror of right fibularis longus.

origin: Lower two-thirds of lateral fibula.
insertion: Tuberosity at base of fifth metatarsal.
action: Eversion of foot; assists plantarflexion.
notes: Shorter of the two fibularis muscles. Inserts on the prominent tuberosity of the fifth metatarsal, which can avulse with inversion ankle sprains.

origin: Lower two-thirds of lateral fibula.
insertion: Base of fifth metatarsal.
action: Foot eversion and plantarflexion.
notes: Mirror of right fibularis brevis.

origin: Middle half of medial surface of fibula; interosseous membrane.
insertion: Dorsal surface of base of distal phalanx of great toe.
action: Extension of great toe; dorsiflexion and inversion of foot.
notes: Lies between tibialis anterior and extensor digitorum longus. Its tendon is visible on the dorsum of the foot during toe extension.

origin: Middle fibula and interosseous membrane.
insertion: Distal phalanx of great toe.
action: Great toe extension; foot dorsiflexion.
notes: Mirror of right extensor hallucis longus.

origin: Medial process of calcaneal tuberosity; plantar aponeurosis.
insertion: Middle phalanges of lateral four toes (splits around flexor digitorum longus tendons).
action: Flexion of middle phalanges of lateral four toes.
notes: First layer of plantar intrinsic foot muscles. Analogous to flexor digitorum superficialis in the hand.

origin: Medial calcaneal tuberosity; plantar aponeurosis.
insertion: Middle phalanges of lateral four toes.
action: Toe flexion (middle phalanges).
notes: Mirror of right flexor digitorum brevis.

origin: Medial process of calcaneal tuberosity; flexor retinaculum; plantar aponeurosis.
insertion: Medial side of base of proximal phalanx of great toe.
action: Abduction and flexion of great toe; supports medial longitudinal arch.
notes: Forms the medial border of the foot. Important in supporting the medial arch and in propulsion during gait.

origin: Medial calcaneal tuberosity and plantar aponeurosis.
insertion: Base of proximal phalanx of great toe.
action: Great toe abduction and flexion; medial arch support.
notes: Mirror of right abductor hallucis.

origin: Humeral head: medial epicondyle (common flexor origin). Ulnar head: coronoid process of ulna.
insertion: Middle of lateral surface of radius.
action: Pronation of forearm; weak flexion of elbow.
notes: The median nerve passes between its two heads. Pronator syndrome involves compression of the median nerve here.

origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus and coronoid process of ulna.
insertion: Lateral radius.
action: Forearm pronation; weak elbow flexion.
notes: Mirror of right pronator teres.

origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus (common flexor origin).
insertion: Base of second (and partly third) metacarpal.
action: Flexion of wrist; radial deviation (abduction) of hand.
notes: Tendon is a useful landmark for radial artery palpation. Runs in its own compartment of the flexor retinaculum.

origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus.
insertion: Base of second metacarpal.
action: Wrist flexion; radial deviation.
notes: Mirror of right flexor carpi radialis.

origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus.
insertion: Flexor retinaculum and palmar aponeurosis.
action: Weak wrist flexion; tenses palmar aponeurosis.
notes: Absent in ~14% of individuals. Used as a tendon graft donor site in reconstructive surgery. Tendon visible centrally at the wrist during active wrist flexion.

origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus.
insertion: Palmar aponeurosis.
action: Weak wrist flexion.
notes: Mirror of right palmaris longus. Absent in ~14% of population.

origin: Humeral head: medial epicondyle. Ulnar head: olecranon and posterior ulna.
insertion: Pisiform, hamate, and base of fifth metacarpal.
action: Flexion of wrist; ulnar deviation of hand.
notes: Only flexor muscle of the forearm innervated by the ulnar nerve. The pisiform acts as a sesamoid bone within its tendon.

origin: Medial epicondyle and olecranon.
insertion: Pisiform, hamate, fifth metacarpal.
action: Wrist flexion; ulnar deviation.
notes: Mirror of right flexor carpi ulnaris.

origin: Humeroulnar head: medial epicondyle and coronoid process. Radial head: anterior oblique line of radius.
insertion: Sides of middle phalanges of medial four fingers (splits to allow FDP through).
action: Flexion of middle phalanges; assists flexion of proximal phalanges and wrist.
notes: The only muscle in the intermediate layer of the forearm. Its tendons split (Camper's chiasma) to allow FDP tendons to pass through to the distal phalanges.

origin: Medial epicondyle, coronoid process, anterior radius.
insertion: Middle phalanges of medial four fingers.
action: Finger PIP flexion; wrist flexion.
notes: Mirror of right FDS.

origin: Lateral supracondylar ridge of humerus.
insertion: Dorsal base of second metacarpal.
action: Extension of wrist; radial deviation of hand.
notes: Crosses the lateral elbow; forms the floor of the anatomical snuffbox proximally. Active in gripping motions to stabilize the wrist in extension.

origin: Lateral supracondylar ridge of humerus.
insertion: Base of second metacarpal.
action: Wrist extension; radial deviation.
notes: Mirror of right ECRL.

origin: Lateral epicondyle of humerus (common extensor origin).
insertion: Extensor expansions of medial four fingers (middle and distal phalanges).
action: Extension of fingers at MCP, PIP, and DIP joints; assists wrist extension.
innervation: Posterior interosseous nerve (deep radial nerve) (C7, C8).
notes: Tendons joined by juncturae tendinum on the dorsum of the hand. Major extensor of the fingers; its tendons form the dorsal extensor expansion.

origin: Lateral epicondyle of humerus.
insertion: Extensor expansions of medial four fingers.
action: Finger extension at all joints; wrist extension.
notes: Mirror of right extensor digitorum.

origin: Epicranial aponeurosis.
insertion: Skin and subcutaneous tissue of the forehead; blends with procerus, corrugator supercilii, and orbicularis oculi.
action: Elevation of eyebrows; wrinkling of forehead skin (surprise expression).
innervation: Temporal branches of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: Anterior belly of the occipitofrontalis muscle. Has no bony attachment — entirely muscle and skin.

origin: Epicranial aponeurosis.
insertion: Forehead skin.
action: Eyebrow elevation; forehead wrinkling.
notes: Mirror of right frontalis.

origin: Orbital part: medial orbital margin and frontal process of maxilla. Palpebral part: medial palpebral ligament.
insertion: Orbital part: lateral palpebral raphe. Palpebral part: lateral palpebral raphe.
action: Orbital part: tight/forceful eye closure. Palpebral part: gentle/voluntary eye closure. Lacrimal part: dilates lacrimal sac.
innervation: Temporal and zygomatic branches of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: Sphincter muscle of the orbit; responsible for all forms of eye closure. Palpebral part involved in blinking; orbital part in tight closure.

origin: Medial orbital margin and medial palpebral ligament.
insertion: Lateral palpebral raphe.
action: Eye closure (gentle and forceful); lacrimal pump.
notes: Mirror of right orbicularis oculi.

origin: Zygomatic bone, posterior to zygomaticotemporal suture.
insertion: Modiolus — the fibromuscular node at the angle of the mouth.
action: Draws angle of mouth superolaterally — the primary muscle of smiling.
innervation: Buccal branches of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: The 'smiling muscle'. Runs from the cheekbone to the corner of the mouth. Often the most visible cheek muscle during expression.

origin: Zygomatic bone.
insertion: Angle of mouth (modiolus).
action: Draws mouth corner superolaterally — smiling.
notes: Mirror of right zygomaticus major.

origin: Superficial fascia overlying pectoralis major and deltoid.
insertion: Lower border of mandible; skin and subcutaneous tissue of lower face and cheek.
action: Draws corner of mouth inferolaterally; tenses skin of neck and lower face; assists in depressing mandible.
innervation: Cervical branch of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: Broad, thin sheet of muscle in the superficial cervical fascia. Visible as vertical neck cords during straining. Surgically important in neck dissection and facelift procedures.

origin: Superficial fascia of pectoral and deltoid region.
insertion: Lower mandible and lower facial skin.
action: Depresses mandible and lower lip; tenses neck skin.
innervation: Cervical branch of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: Mirror of right platysma.

origin: Modiolus and adjacent maxilla and mandible; fibers from surrounding facial muscles.
insertion: Skin and mucosa of lips; blends circumferentially.
action: Closes and protrudes lips; compresses lips against teeth; shapes lips for speech and drinking.
innervation: Buccal and marginal mandibular branches of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: Complex sphincter of the oral fissure. Not a simple circular muscle — composed of intrinsic and extrinsic fibers from many other facial muscles.

origin: Alveolar processes of maxilla and mandible (opposite molar teeth); pterygomandibular raphe.
insertion: Modiolus — blends into orbicularis oris.
action: Compresses cheek against teeth; keeps food in contact with teeth during chewing; blowing/whistling.
innervation: Buccal branches of facial nerve (CN VII); sensory by buccal nerve (CN V3).
notes: Deep facial muscle of the cheek. Parotid duct pierces it. Essential in chewing, blowing instruments, and whistling.

origin: Alveolar processes of maxilla and mandible; pterygomandibular raphe.
insertion: Modiolus.
action: Cheek compression; chewing assistance.
notes: Mirror of right buccinator.

origin: Incisive fossa of mandible.
insertion: Skin of chin.
action: Raises and protrudes lower lip; wrinkles chin skin (pouting expression).
innervation: Marginal mandibular branch of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: The only muscle of the chin. Its contraction produces the characteristic puckered or pouting chin appearance.

origin: Medial superciliary arch (superomedial orbital margin).
insertion: Skin of medial half of eyebrow.
action: Draws eyebrow medially and inferiorly — produces vertical frown lines.
innervation: Temporal branch of facial nerve (CN VII).
notes: The frowning muscle. Lies deep to frontalis and orbicularis oculi. Target of botulinum toxin for glabellar lines.

origin: Medial superciliary arch.
insertion: Skin of medial eyebrow.
action: Draws eyebrow medially and down — frowning.
notes: Mirror of right corrugator supercilii.
